_comment: Definitions sourced from Dragon's Dogma 1 dialogue (combined_output.csv)

dd1_definitions.in truth: indeed; truly; in reality; verily (adverbial phrase used to emphasize the truth of a statement or to assert sincerity
dd1_definitions.very well: all right; okay; certainly; excellently (/formal expression of agreement:  consent:  or acknowledgment
dd1_definitions.amidst: in the middle of; among; surrounded by (preposition variant of 'amid'; in the midst of)
dd1_definitions.aught: anything at all; anything whatever; any part:  amount:  or degree (pronoun/noun:  often in negative constructions meaning 'nothing'; also variant of 'ought' as zero from misdivision of 'a naught')
dd1_definitions.sunder: to separate; to divide; to split apart (verb); 'in sunder' or 'asunder' meaning apart or into pieces (: to put asunder:  rend; common in )
art: The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements,...
Stay and learn the ***art*** of war.
dd1_definitions.are they not: rhetorical/tag question form (equivalent to modern 'aren't they?')
dd1_definitions.magnificent: grand; splendid; noble; excellent (often with connotations of pomp or show; imposing in appearance)
dd1_definitions.scarce: scarcely; barely; hardly; with difficulty (adverbial use; also adjective meaning rare or insufficient)
dd1_definitions.betwixt: between (preposition; also 'betwixt and between' meaning in an intermediate position)
dd1_definitions.fare well: farewell; goodbye; may you fare well (parting expression; variant of 'farewell')
dd1_definitions.behest: command; order; injunction; request (noun; : a command or authoritative bidding)
dd1_definitions.wight: person; human being; creature (noun:  often with 'poor wight' meaning unfortunate person)
dd1_definitions.bane: cause of misery or death; poison; ruin; curse (noun; something that causes harm or destruction)
dd1_definitions.tarry: to wait; to delay; to linger; to stay (verb; : to remain or wait)
dd1_definitions.dire: dreadful; terrible; ominous; causing horror (adjective; horrible)
dd1_definitions.I know not: I know not (for 'I do not know'; common  inversion)
dd1_definitions.lest: for fear that; in order to prevent; that...not (conjunction)
forth: Forward in time, place or degree.
hence: From here, from this place, away
Let them live now and we'll be plagued ***hence***. This ends here!
dd1_definitions.henceforth: from this time forward; from now on (adverb)
dd1_definitions.whence: from where; from what place/source; how (adverb/conjunction)
dd1_definitions.fain: gladly; willingly; with pleasure; compelled or obliged (adverb/adjective; : gladly:  with pleasure)
dd1_definitions.goodly: handsome; fine; pleasant; considerable in size (adjective/adverb)
dd1_definitions.farewell: goodbye; a parting wish of well-being (interjection/noun)
dd1_definitions.must: must (modal verb with same senses as modern: but often in formal or emphatic Early Modern usage)
dd1_definitions.hail: to greet; to call to; hail (verb/interjection of greeting)
dd1_definitions.smite: to strike; to hit forcefully; to afflict or punish (verb; to strike with a weapon)
dd1_definitions.jest: joke; to mock or jeer; a witty remark (noun/verb; also 'in jest' meaning not seriously)
dd1_definitions.lo: Look, see, behold (in an imperative sense; interjection)
dd1_definitions.nay: no; not; indeed (adverb for negation or emphasis)
dd1_definitions.aye: yes; ever; always (adverb for affirmation or eternity)
behold: To see or look at, esp. appreciatively; to descry, look upon.
dd1_definitions.fie: shame; for shame (interjection of disgust or reproach)
anon: I shall see you at the abbey ***anon***. Maker speed your steps.
dd1_definitions.queer: strange; odd; peculiar (also 'to queer' meaning to spoil)
dd1_definitions.whither: to where; to what place; whereto (adverb/conjunction)
dd1_definitions.wish to: desire to; want to (periphrastic for future/intention in Early Modern English)
dd1_definitions.was not: did not exist or occur (past negative of 'was'; replaces 'wasn't')
dd1_definitions.were not: did not exist or occur (past negative of 'were'; replaces 'weren't')
dd1_definitions.were you not: tag question form (replaces 'weren't you?'; archaic phrasing)
dd1_definitions.whate'er: whatever; whatsoever; anything that (poetic contraction; replaces 'whatever')
dd1_definitions.whene'er: whenever; at any time; whensoever (poetic contraction; replaces 'whenever')
dd1_definitions.where'er: wherever; in any place; wheresoever (poetic contraction; replaces 'wherever')
wilt: The act of wilting or the state of being wilted.
Even the loveliest flowers ***wilt*** in time, master.
ye: Address a single person by the use of the pronoun ye instead of thou.
dd1_definitions.assay: to try; to test; to attempt (verb/noun)
dd1_definitions.commend: to entrust; to praise; to recommend (verb)
dd1_definitions.demesne: A lord's chief manor place, with that part of the lands belonging thereto (noun; domain, estate)
dd1_definitions.vanquish: to conquer; to defeat; to overcome (verb)
dd1_definitions.yon: over there; that place in the distance (adjective/adverb; variant of 'yonder')
dd1_definitions.with all speed: quickly; as fast as possible (adverbial phrase)
wit: Ah, hello... As you can like tell from the look of me, I'm at my ***wit***'s end.
dd1_definitions.beseech: to beg; to implore; to request earnestly (verb)
dd1_definitions.cease: to stop; to end; to discontinue (verb); end or stopping point (noun)
dd1_definitions.quell: to subdue; to suppress; to put down; to conquer; to calm (verb; to put an end to by force)
dd1_definitions.apart: separated; away from; into pieces; aside (adverb/adjective; separated from others)
dd1_definitions.awesome: Inspiring awe; especially:  extremely impressive or daunting;
dd1_definitions.awful: Inspiring awe; especially:  extremely impressive or daunting.
dd1_definitions.soil: to stain; to defile; to make dirty (verb; archaic sense: to stain or dishonor, not the modern sense of earth)
dd1_definitions.in short: in summary; briefly; to put it briefly (adverbial phrase used as a formal replacement for longer explanations or modern contractions)
dd1_definitions.abyssal: relating to the abyss; bottomless; immeasurably deep (adjective)
dd1_definitions.acquisitor: one who acquires; a gatherer or collector (noun; from DD1 item names)
dd1_definitions.alluvial: relating to alluvium; deposited by water; sedimentary (adjective)
dd1_definitions.ambrosial: delightful; delicious; sweet-tasting (adjective; like ambrosia, food of gods)
dd1_definitions.anathema: something accursed; a formal ban or curse; detested thing (noun)
dd1_definitions.argence: silver; silver-colored (adjective; poetic/archaic term for silver)
dd1_definitions.auspicious: favorable; propitious; showing signs of future success (adjective)
dd1_definitions.avernal: infernal; hellish; relating to the underworld (adjective)
dd1_definitions.azure: bright blue; sky blue (adjective/noun; heraldic color)
dd1_definitions.chamberlain: officer in charge of managing a household; steward (noun)
dd1_definitions.decoction: extract obtained by boiling; herbal brew (noun)
dd1_definitions.distilled: purified by distillation; extracted by heating and condensing (adjective/participle)
ebon: The ***ebon*** armor absorbed the light, rendering the wearer nearly invisible in shadow.
dd1_definitions.infernal: hellish; diabolical; belonging to the underworld (adjective)
dd1_definitions.interventive: curative; healing; medicinal (adjective; from DD1 items)
dd1_definitions.lodestone: something that strongly attracts, magnet; magnetic stone (noun)
dd1_definitions.mandrake: poisonous plant with human-shaped root; magical herb (noun)
dd1_definitions.martyr: one who suffers for a cause; person killed for beliefs (noun)
dd1_definitions.mitigator: one who mitigates; reducer of severity (noun)
dd1_definitions.petrifactor: something that turns to stone; petrifying agent (noun)
dd1_definitions.scarlet: A brilliant red colour tinged with orange (adjective/noun)
virid: The ***virid*** moss glowed softly in the cavern, the only light in the darkness.

_comment2: Definitions sourced from non-DD1 sources (API, manual curation, etc.)

other_definitions.art: second person singular present of 'to be'; thou art = you are (verb; archaic form for 'you are')
other_definitions.wilt: second person singular future of 'will'; thou wilt = you will (verb; archaic form for 'you will')
other_definitions.belike: perhaps; probably; it may be; likely (adverb meaning 'it is likely')
other_definitions.ifsoe'er: if ever (contraction/variant of 'if so e'er' or 'ifsoever'; poetic for 'if ever at all')
other_definitions.haply: perhaps; by chance; possibly; luckily (adverb; : by hap or chance)
other_definitions.betimes: early; soon; in good time; occasionally (adverb)
other_definitions.forsooth: indeed; truly; in truth (adverb used often ironically or for emphasis)
other_definitions.methinks: it seems to me; I think (impersonal verb)
other_definitions.'twere: it were (subjunctive contraction; archaic form of 'it were')
other_definitions.most wise: very wise; wisest (adjective with superlative meaning)
other_definitions.prithee: I pray thee; please (contraction of 'I pray thee')
other_definitions.wend: to go; to proceed; to turn (verb)
other_definitions.bethink: to think; to consider; to devise; to remember (verb; to think of:  consider:  devise)
other_definitions.coz: cousin (abbreviation; used for any relative or intimate)
other_definitions.forfend: to forbid; to avert; to protect from (verb)
thence: From there, from that place or from that time.
He went to the village and ***thence*** returned home.
hither: Come ***hither***, child, and let me see what manner of creature has wounded you so.
other_definitions.recreant: coward; traitor; one who yields; false (noun/adjective)
other_definitions.troth: truth; fidelity; pledge; betrothal (noun; 'by my troth' = truly)
other_definitions.yonder: over there; that place in the distance (adverb/adjective)
e'en: Even the strongest warrior may fall ***e'en*** to a single blow.
other_definitions.wit not: have no knowledge (phrase; 'wit not' = I do not know)
other_definitions.bespeak: to speak to; to request; to indicate (verb)
other_definitions.sup: to eat or drink (verb); a meal (noun)
other_definitions.resemble: to be like or similar to (something); to represent as similar (verb)
fleet: A group of vessels or vehicles.
might: Power, strength, force or influence held by a person or group.
anew: Again, once more; afresh, in a new way, newly.
Each morning, opportunity—like the sun—dawns anew.
whilst: (rare or literary in North America) While, at the same time.
Drivers must switch off engines whilst on stand.
